mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer spending and corporate revenue expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth in the top quartile does not produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of subsequent months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.0% reflects sufficiently restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) and a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signal an overheating economy that forces the Federal Reserve into hawkish policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Joint increases in CPIAUCSL and decreases in UNRATE do not produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of joint-signal months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth (INDPRO) forecasts positive S&P 500 returns only when the economy operates near full employment, because capacity-constrained output gains translate into pricing power and margin expansion rather than just volume.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, positive monthly changes in INDPRO are followed by negative 5-day forward returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in unemployment (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating household income and demand destruction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: After UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in 3 months, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Growth in new housing starts (HOUST) leads positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon because residential investment multiplies through construction, durable goods, and credit channels.
failure_condition: HOUST in its top tercile does not outperform the bottom tercile on 60-day forward returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth (RSAFS) accelerates S&P 500 returns only when payroll employment (PAYEMS) is simultaneously expanding, because income-supported spending is more durable than credit-driven consumption.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of joint-signal weeks.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth (GDPC1) surprises validate the persistence of aggregate demand and corporate pricing power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Above-median GDPC1 growth does not correspond to positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of quarters.
